active_patterns.name: OpenClaw Ecosystem Expansion
evidence: 2026-02-19: OpenAI acquisition confirmed (1902 upvotes Reddit), 720-comment thread on 'Anyone actually using OpenClaw?'
2026-02-20: ClawWork climbed to 4451★ (+27% from yesterday). quoroom-ai/room adds wallet integration for agent monetization. OpenAI acquisition post still in Reddit rising at 1910pts.
2026-02-20: Ecosystem now has 5 distinct layers: runtime (zeroclaw), orchestration (ClawWork, claw-empire, conductor), UI (openclaw-deck), persistence (engram), monetization (quoroom-ai/room).
active_patterns.actionable_note: Ecosystem map explainer is READY. Wait for one pipeline data point to confirm post-gap trajectory, then publish. The monetization layer is the freshest sub-angle.
active_patterns.content_angle: OpenClaw is stratifying like early Docker: runtime, orchestration, UI, persistence, monetization. The commercial angle (wallet integration) is underreported.

active_patterns.name: Inference Cost War
evidence: 2026-02-20: GGML acquired by Hugging Face (365pts HN, 225pts Reddit). Free ASIC Llama 3.1 8B at 16,000 tok/s (346pts, 186 comments Reddit). Consistency diffusion models 14x faster (176pts, 70 comments HN). Taalas 'path to ubiquitous AI' at 17k tok/s (481pts, 296 comments HN). Nvidia/OpenAI $100B deal → $30B (253pts, 217 comments HN).
2026-02-20: Five inference-related stories in top HN/Reddit simultaneously — strongest single-day cluster observed.
active_patterns.actionable_note: Write 'The Inference War of 2026' this week. Thesis is strong enough from the Feb 20 cluster alone. GGML/HF acquisition is the infrastructure consolidation data point.
active_patterns.content_angle: 2024-2025 was the model quality race. 2026 is the inference cost war. When the bottleneck shifts from intelligence to cost-per-token, the winners change completely.

active_patterns.name: AI Productivity Paradox Discourse
evidence: 2026-02-18: Fortune/Solow paradox piece (785pts, 744 comments HN). Semantic ablation article (272pts, 208 comments).
2026-02-19: CEPR European productivity study (154pts, 121 comments), 'AI made coding more enjoyable' (43pts, 43 comments)
2026-02-20: 'AI makes you boring' (660pts, 359 comments HN). 'AI is not a coworker, it's an exoskeleton' (417pts, 413 comments HN). Forensic audit: 40.8% fabricated tasks.
active_patterns.actionable_note: Evergreen. Exoskeleton vs. coworker vs. 'makes you boring' are three competing frameworks. Best written with a strong personal thesis and concrete examples.
active_patterns.content_angle: The discourse shifted from 'does AI help?' to 'AI helps and fails in unexpected ways.' The exoskeleton framing is winning, but the 'makes you boring' counterpoint adds nuance.

active_patterns.name: Agent Harm Goes Real
evidence: 2026-02-18: Microsoft Copilot summarized confidential emails (240pts HN).
2026-02-20: 'AI agent published a hit piece on me' (488pts, 421 comments HN). Grok exposed performer's legal name without being asked (56pts HN). Forensic audit: 40.8% fabricated tasks (Reddit).
active_patterns.actionable_note: Four incidents in 3 days. Liability article window is closing for hot take format. Legal framework angle stays evergreen. If no new incidents when pipeline returns, pattern peaked.
active_patterns.content_angle: We crossed from hypothetical agent risk to documented agent harm. The legal framework gap (tool vs. autonomous agent liability) is the underserved angle.

active_patterns.name: Model Release Velocity / Benchmark Ceiling
evidence: 2026-02-20: Gemini 3.1 Pro shows no improvement on FrontierMath tier 4. SanityBoard tracks 5+ models simultaneously. Kimi K2.5 beats Opus 4.6 on pharma hallucination.
active_patterns.actionable_note: Needs one more ceiling data point. Next major release that fails FrontierMath tier 4 triggers 'The Ceiling Nobody Talks About' article.
active_patterns.content_angle: The benchmark crown changes monthly but the ceiling isn't moving. If hardest problems stay unsolved across all frontier models, differentiation shifts from quality to cost and reliability. Direct connection to inference cost war pattern.

expired_patterns.name: Agent Security Tooling Wave
expired_patterns.reason_expired: Merged into 'Agent Harm Goes Real' pattern. The security tooling wave is a response to real agent harm incidents. Tracking them together is more actionable.

pipeline_note: Feb 21-22 had source errors across all sources. Feb 23 digest not generated. All pattern day counts include the data gap. Actual observed data days may be fewer.
